description: This figure presents the Time-Temperature-Transformation (TTT) diagram for γ solid solution decomposition during isothermal aging of Fe-28Mn-8.5Al-1C-1.25Si low-density steel. The x-axis is isothermal time in logarithmic scale ranging from 0.01 h to 1000 h, and the y-axis is aging temperature from 300 °C to 1000 °C. Five distinct phase regions are marked: Region 1 is single γ phase corresponding to spinodal decomposition; Region 2 is γ + κ' phase region, where homogeneous κ' carbide precipitates from the matrix; Region 3 is γ + κ' + κ* phase region, corresponding to grain boundary κ* carbide formation; Region 4 is γ + κ' + κ* + α(B2/DO₃) phase region, representing discontinuous grain boundary reaction products of B2/DO₃ structured α phase; Region 5 is γ + κ + B2/DO₃ phase region, where equilibrium phases form. The right side indicates ordered phase stability ranges: B2 phase at temperatures above ~750 °C, mixed B2 + DO₃ phase at intermediate temperatures, and DO₃ phase below ~650 °C. This diagram clarifies phase evolution paths under different isothermal aging conditions, providing guidance for heat treatment design to control microstructures of the alloy.
